activities.d: Dubai dazzles with the world's tallest building, the Burj Khalifa, plus luxury shopping and futuristic architecture.
A desert safari of dune-bashing, camel rides and a Bedouin-style dinner is the classic Dubai add-on.
Dubai pairs warm-water beaches with mega-malls — one even has an indoor ski slope.
Dubai's nightlife runs to glitzy rooftop lounges and beach clubs (alcohol served in licensed venues).

hazards.note: Extreme heat — daytime highs of 105-115F / 40-46C

seasonal.d: Nov-March is the comfortable season for beaches and outdoor sightseeing, before the extreme summer heat.

AE.summary: Skyscrapers, shopping, desert safaris and beaches.

activities.d: Band-e Amir, Afghanistan's first national park, is a chain of startlingly blue mountain lakes.
Herat's ancient citadel and minarets reflect its golden age on the Silk Road.
Kabul's museums and bazaars hold the threads of a long, layered history.
The remote Wakhan Corridor is a stunning high-mountain finger between the Pamirs and Hindu Kush.

seasonal.d: Spring and autumn historically offered the mildest weather — though travel here carries serious safety risks today.

AF.summary: Hindu Kush scenery and ancient Silk Road heritage — under a Do Not Travel advisory; effectively closed to tourism.

activities.d: The Albanian Riviera has turquoise coves and beaches rivalling its neighbours, at a fraction of the price.
Berat and Gjirokaster are UNESCO 'museum cities' of tiered Ottoman stone houses.
Theth and the 'Accursed Mountains' offer dramatic alpine hiking and stone-tower villages.
Tirana is a colourful, fast-changing capital with a lively cafe and bar scene.

seasonal.d: Jun-Sep is beach season on the Riviera — warm, sunny and increasingly popular.
Jun-Sep is the season for hiking the Albanian Alps, including the famous Valbona-to-Theth trail.

AL.summary: Riviera beaches and mountain villages at Balkan prices.

activities.d: Armenia's ancient monasteries — cliff-carved Geghard and Tatev, reached by the world's longest cable car — are spectacular.
Yerevan is a cafe city famous for its brandy, with Mount Ararat looming on the horizon.
Lake Sevan, a vast high-altitude lake, is Armenia's summer beach and a monastery-topped peninsula.
Mount Aragats, Armenia's highest peak, offers alpine meadows and rewarding hikes.

seasonal.d: Late spring and early autumn are the mildest for sightseeing across Armenia.
Summer (Jun-Sep) opens the high meadows and lakeshore for hiking.

AM.summary: Ancient monasteries, mountain scenery and brandy in one of the world's oldest Christian nations.

activities.d: The Kalandula Falls are among Africa's largest — a thunderous, little-visited spectacle.
Iona National Park is a remote desert wilderness where dunes meet the Atlantic, akin to Namibia.
Luanda, the lively (and famously pricey) capital, curves around a palm-lined bay.
Kissama National Park near Luanda is being restocked into Angola's flagship safari park.

seasonal.d: May-Oct (the cool, dry season) is the most practical time to travel.

AO.summary: Vast empty beaches, desert meeting ocean, and rebounding wildlife.

activities.d: Buenos Aires is all about late-night steakhouses, Malbec and sultry tango in barrios like San Telmo.
El Chalten is Argentina's trekking capital, beneath the jagged Fitz Roy peaks in Patagonia.
Mendoza is the heart of Argentine wine — Malbec tastings with the snow-capped Andes as a backdrop.
The Argentine side of Iguazu Falls puts you right above the Devil's Throat on walkways over the cascades.

seasonal.d: Patagonia's trekking season is the southern summer, roughly November-March, when the weather is mildest.
Jun-Dec, southern right whales gather to breed off Peninsula Valdes on the Atlantic coast.

AR.summary: Buenos Aires, Patagonia trekking, wine country and glaciers.

activities.d: Vienna's Habsburg palaces — Schonbrunn and the Hofburg — recall its imperial grandeur.
Salzburg and the lake-and-mountain village of Hallstatt are Austria's most scenic spots.
The Tyrol is a premier ski region, with charming resort towns like Innsbruck and Kitzbuhel.
Vienna's grand coffee houses and classical-music heritage (Mozart, the New Year's Concert) define its culture.

seasonal.d: Summer (Jun-Sep) is for Alpine hiking and swimming in the clear Salzkammergut lakes.
December-April is ski season in the Tyrolean and Arlberg Alps.
Vienna and Salzburg host some of Europe's most atmospheric Advent Christmas markets in December.

AT.summary: Vienna's music and cafes, alpine lakes and ski villages.

activities.d: The Great Barrier Reef is the world's largest coral reef system — snorkel or dive it from Cairns or the Whitsundays.
Sydney pairs its Opera House and Harbour Bridge with famous city beaches like Bondi.
The Red Centre's Uluru, a vast sacred monolith, glows red at sunrise and sunset in the heart of the outback.
Melbourne is Australia's coffee-and-culture capital, near the Great Ocean Road and Yarra Valley wineries.

hazards.note: Cyclone and box-jellyfish season in the tropical north (Cairns, Darwin)

seasonal.d: Jun-Oct is the dry season — calm, clear water and the best reef visibility.
May-Nov, humpback whales migrate along the east coast, spotted from Sydney to Hervey Bay.

AU.summary: Reef and beaches, the Outback, and big coastal cities.

activities.d: Baku contrasts a walled medieval old city with the gleaming Flame Towers on the Caspian.
Gobustan has ancient petroglyphs and a cluster of gurgling mud volcanoes.
activities.p: Gobustan State Historical and Cultural Reserve

activities.d: Sheki, with its ornate khan's palace, is a gateway to scenic Caucasus mountain villages.
Baku's landscaped Caspian seafront boulevard is the heart of city life.

seasonal.d: Spring and autumn are the most comfortable seasons for Baku and the countryside.

AZ.summary: Baku's old city and flame towers, mud volcanoes and Caucasus villages.

activities.d: Mostar's iconic arched Stari Most bridge leaps an emerald river above an Ottoman old bazaar.
Sarajevo, where East meets West, layers Ottoman, Austro-Hungarian and recent wartime history.
The Kravice waterfalls are a wide, swimmable curtain of cascades near Mostar.
The Tara River canyon, Europe's deepest, offers thrilling white-water rafting.

seasonal.d: Late spring to early autumn is best for the old towns, waterfalls and rafting.
Dec-Mar, the 1984 Olympic mountains Jahorina and Bjelasnica offer some of Europe's cheapest skiing.

BA.summary: Ottoman bridges, riverside old towns and raw Dinaric Alps scenery at low prices.

activities.d: The Sundarbans is the world's largest mangrove forest and home to the elusive Bengal tiger.
Dhaka is a riot of colour and rickshaws, with the river boats of Sadarghat at its heart.
Cox's Bazar claims one of the world's longest unbroken natural sand beaches.
Sylhet's rolling green tea gardens are Bangladesh's most scenic landscape.

hazards.note: Monsoon — widespread flooding is common

seasonal.d: Nov-Feb is the cool, dry season — by far the most comfortable time to travel.

BD.summary: River-delta life, the world's longest natural beach and the Sundarbans mangroves.

activities.d: Bruges and Ghent are exquisitely preserved medieval cities of canals, gabled houses and belfries.
Belgium is a world capital of beer and chocolate — sample both around Brussels's grand market square.
Antwerp blends Rubens-era art with a cutting-edge fashion and diamond scene.
Brussels has a relaxed bar scene built around hundreds of distinctive Belgian beers.

seasonal.d: Late spring to early autumn is the most pleasant time for Belgium's walkable cities.
December brings festive Christmas markets and lights to Brussels and Bruges.

BE.summary: Medieval squares, beer, chocolate and easy train hops.

activities.d: Bobo-Dioulasso's mud-brick grand mosque anchors the country's most atmospheric old quarter.
Burkina Faso is a West African craft hub for bronze, masks and woven textiles.
Ouagadougou hosts FESPACO, the biggest film festival in Africa.
The Sindou Peaks are a surreal range of eroded, fin-like rock formations.

seasonal.d: Nov-Feb is the cooler dry season, the most comfortable time to travel.

BF.summary: West African crafts, music and mud-brick architecture — currently under a Do Not Travel advisory.

activities.d: Bulgaria's Black Sea coast has some of Europe's best-value beach resorts, like Sunny Beach.
The UNESCO-listed Rila Monastery, with its striped arches set in the mountains, is Bulgaria's spiritual heart.
Plovdiv, one of Europe's oldest cities, has a charming old town and a Roman amphitheatre.
Bansko is Bulgaria's top, budget-friendly ski resort in the Pirin Mountains.

seasonal.d: Jun-Sep is Black Sea beach season — warm, sunny and inexpensive.
Dec-Mar is ski season in resorts like Bansko.

BG.summary: Black Sea beaches, monasteries and cheap ski slopes.

activities.d: Lake Tanganyika's sandy beaches near Bujumbura are Burundi's main leisure spot.
Gishora is the home of Burundi's UNESCO-listed sacred royal drumming.
Bujumbura, on the lakeshore, is the laid-back former capital.
Kibira National Park is a montane rainforest with primates and waterfalls.

seasonal.d: Jun-Sep is the long dry season — the easiest time to travel.

BI.summary: Lake Tanganyika shores, drumming traditions and green highlands.

activities.d: Ganvie is a remarkable lake village of stilt houses, sometimes called the 'Venice of Africa'.
Ouidah is the spiritual home of Vodun (voodoo) and a key stop on the historic Slave Route.
The royal palaces of Abomey recall the once-powerful Kingdom of Dahomey.
Pendjari National Park is one of West Africa's best, with elephants, lions and antelope.

seasonal.d: Nov-Mar is the dry season — the best time to travel.
January 10th is the national Vodun Festival, centred on Ouidah.

BJ.summary: Birthplace of vodun, stilt villages and the Slave Route's poignant history.

activities.d: Brunei's capital is dominated by opulent gold-domed mosques like the Omar Ali Saifuddien.
Kampong Ayer is a centuries-old 'water village' of stilted houses on the river.
Ulu Temburong National Park offers a rainforest canopy walkway deep in pristine jungle.
The Royal Regalia Museum displays the sultan's lavish ceremonial treasures.

seasonal.d: Brunei is wet year-round; Jan-Apr tends to be the relatively drier window.

BN.summary: Gilded mosques, water villages and pristine Bornean rainforest in a tiny sultanate.

activities.d: The Salar de Uyuni is the world's largest salt flat — a blinding white expanse that mirrors the sky when wet.
La Paz, the world's highest capital, spills dramatically down a canyon, linked by aerial cable cars.
Bolivia's side of Lake Titicaca includes the sacred Isla del Sol, the legendary Inca birthplace.
Rurrenabaque is the gateway to Amazon and pampas wildlife tours in Bolivia's lowlands.

hazards.note: Altiplano rainy season — salt-flat and back-road travel gets difficult

seasonal.d: May-Oct is the dry season — clear skies and the easiest travel across the altiplano.
In the wet months (roughly Jan-Mar) a thin water layer turns the salt flat into a giant mirror.

BO.summary: The Uyuni salt flats, high-altitude cities and Lake Titicaca.

activities.d: Rio's Copacabana and Ipanema beaches sit beneath Christ the Redeemer and Sugarloaf Mountain.
Rio and Salvador throw the world's biggest Carnival and keep up samba-fuelled nightlife year-round.
Manaus is the gateway to Amazon riverboat trips and jungle lodges deep in the rainforest.
Iguazu Falls is a thundering 2.7km wall of cascades through the jungle on the Argentine border.

seasonal.d: Carnival explodes for the days before Lent (Feb or early March) — Brazil's wildest celebration.
December-March is Brazilian summer — hot, festive beach season around New Year and Carnival.
The dry season (roughly Jul-Oct) is prime for spotting jaguars and wildlife in the Pantanal wetlands.

BR.summary: Rio beaches, the Amazon, Iguazú Falls and Carnival.

activities.d: The Exuma cays are home to the famous swimming pigs and impossibly clear water.
Nassau and Paradise Island hold the Bahamas' big resorts and beaches, a short hop from Florida.
The Bahamas' deep blue holes and reefs make for world-class diving and snorkeling.
The quiet Out Islands offer pink-sand beaches and barely-there crowds.

hazards.note: Peak hurricane season

seasonal.d: Dec-Apr is the dry, peak season — sunny, calm and ideal for the water.
Jun-Nov is hurricane season, with the highest risk Aug-Oct, so watch forecasts.

BS.summary: Swimming pigs, blue holes and 700 islands of postcard Caribbean.

activities.d: The Tiger's Nest monastery clings to a cliff high above the Paro valley — Bhutan's iconic hike.
Bhutan offers pristine Himalayan treks, from day walks to the demanding Snowman Trek.
Massive fortress-monasteries (dzongs) host colourful masked tsechu festivals.
Bhutan famously measures Gross National Happiness — its serene valleys and Buddhist culture are the draw.

hazards.note: Monsoon — views disappear and trails turn muddy

seasonal.d: Spring (Mar-May) and autumn (Sep-Nov) bring clear mountain views and the major festivals.

BT.summary: Cliffside monasteries and Himalayan trails in the kingdom of Gross National Happiness (daily visitor fee applies).

activities.d: The Okavango Delta is a vast inland water-wilderness explored by mokoro canoe and on safari.
Chobe National Park hosts one of Africa's largest elephant populations, best seen on a river cruise.
The Kalahari offers stark desert safaris and time with San (Bushmen) communities.
The Makgadikgadi salt pans are among the world's largest — surreal, shimmering emptiness.

hazards.note: Rainy season — game viewing is at its hardest

seasonal.d: May-Oct is the dry season — paradoxically when the Delta floods and game viewing peaks.

BW.summary: The Okavango Delta — Africa's great water safari.

activities.d: Minsk is a showcase of monumental Stalinist architecture along grand boulevards.
The UNESCO-listed Mir and Nesvizh castles are Belarus's most beautiful historic sites.
Bialowieza Forest, shared with Poland, shelters Europe's largest herd of wild bison.
Belarus, devastated in WWII, has powerful memorials like the Brest Fortress and Khatyn.

seasonal.d: May-Sep brings the mildest, greenest weather, well clear of the long winter.

BY.summary: Soviet-era boulevards, castles and primeval forest — currently under a Do Not Travel advisory.

activities.d: Belize's Great Blue Hole is a legendary deep-blue sinkhole on the world's second-largest barrier reef.
Belize's jungle hides Mayan sites like towering Caracol and the riverside Lamanai.
The Actun Tunichil Muknal cave is an awe-inspiring Mayan ceremonial site, reached by wading and swimming.
Inland Belize offers rainforest lodges, cave-tubing and zip-lines in the Cayo district.

hazards.note: Peak hurricane season

seasonal.d: Late Nov-Apr is the dry season — the best window for the reef and the interior.
Around the spring full moons (Mar-Jun), whale sharks gather off Placencia.

BZ.summary: The Belize Barrier Reef, cays and jungle ruins.

activities.d: Banff and Lake Louise in the Rockies offer turquoise lakes, glaciers and world-class hiking.
Toronto and Vancouver are diverse, food-rich cities — Vancouver framed by mountains and sea.
Niagara Falls thunders on the US border, with boat trips right to the base.
Montreal blends French-Canadian flair with festivals and a famous late-night scene.

seasonal.d: Jun-Sep is the window for Rockies hiking, once the high trails clear of snow.
Aug-Apr, the dark northern skies of the Yukon and Northwest Territories are prime for the aurora.

CA.summary: Rockies, lakes, wildlife and northern lights.

activities.d: Virunga, Africa's oldest national park, offers mountain-gorilla trekking amid volcanoes.
Nyiragongo is an overnight climb to peer into the world's largest lava lake, glowing red at night.
The mighty Congo River is the country's great artery of barges, fishing and trade.
Lola ya Bonobo near Kinshasa is the world's only sanctuary for orphaned bonobos.

seasonal.d: The drier months in the east (roughly Jun-Sep and Dec-Feb) are best for trekking (security permitting).

CD.summary: Gorillas and Nyiragongo's lava lake in Africa's wildest park — largely off-limits under a Do Not Travel advisory.

activities.d: Dzanga-Sangha's forest clearing draws dozens of rare forest elephants into the open — a magical sight.
Dzanga-Sangha offers some of Africa's best western lowland gorilla tracking.
The Sangha River winds through dense rainforest where three countries meet.
Forest walks with the BaAka people reveal their deep rainforest knowledge.

seasonal.d: Dec-Apr (the dry season) is the most practical time for the rainforest (security permitting).

CF.summary: Forest elephants and lowland gorillas at Dzanga-Sangha — under a Do Not Travel advisory.

activities.d: Odzala-Kokoua National Park offers superb western lowland gorilla tracking and rainforest lodges.
Brazzaville sits across the Congo River from Kinshasa, the world's closest pair of capitals.
The Lesio-Louna reserve rehabilitates orphaned gorillas within reach of the capital.
Forest clearings (bais) draw elephants, gorillas and buffalo into rare open views.

seasonal.d: Jun-Sep is the main dry season — the best window for the rainforest parks.

CG.summary: Gorillas and untouched rainforest along the other Congo's riverbanks.

activities.d: The Swiss Alps offer world-class hiking beneath icons like the Matterhorn at Zermatt and the Jungfrau at Interlaken.
Switzerland's panoramic trains, like the Glacier Express, are sightseeing in themselves.
Lakeside Lucerne, with its covered wooden bridge and mountain backdrop, is the classic Swiss postcard.
St Moritz and Zermatt are blue-chip ski resorts, with glacier runs open even in summer.

seasonal.d: Roughly June-September is Alpine hiking season, once the snow clears from the high trails.
December-April is ski season across the Swiss Alps, with glacier areas open longer.

CH.summary: Alps, lakes, scenic trains, hiking and skiing.

activities.d: Assinie is Abidjan's weekend beach escape, with lagoons and gentle surf.
Yamoussoukro's basilica is one of the largest churches in the world, modelled on St Peter's.
Abidjan, the buzzing economic capital, is famed for its coupe-decale nightlife.
The Man region offers green hills, waterfalls and traditional liana vine bridges.

hazards.note: Peak rainy season

seasonal.d: Nov-Mar is the drier season — best for the beaches and travel.

CI.summary: Surf beaches, the world's biggest basilica and Abidjan's music scene.
gallery: Basilica of Our Lady of Peace

activities.d: Torres del Paine in Chilean Patagonia is a trekking mecca of granite spires, glaciers and turquoise lakes.
The Atacama, the world's driest desert, has otherworldly salt flats, geysers and the clearest night skies on Earth.
The Casablanca and Maipo valleys near Santiago produce Chile's celebrated wines, easy to tour.
Remote Easter Island is dotted with the enigmatic giant stone moai statues.

seasonal.d: Nov-Mar (southern summer) is the trekking window for Torres del Paine, with the longest days.
The Atacama's bone-dry skies make it a premier stargazing spot all year round.

activities.d: Mount Cameroon, West Africa's highest peak, is a strenuous but accessible volcano climb.
Kribi has palm-fringed beaches and the unusual Lobe Falls, which tumble straight into the sea.
Waza National Park in the far north is Cameroon's best-known savanna safari.
The Ring Road loops the lush, cool Northwest highlands of villages, crater lakes and chiefdoms.

seasonal.d: Nov-Feb is the dry season — the best time to travel.

CM.summary: "Africa in miniature": volcano hikes, beaches, rainforest and Sahel in one country.

activities.d: Sections of the Great Wall near Beijing, like Mutianyu, snake spectacularly over the mountains.
Beijing's Forbidden City, the vast former imperial palace, opens onto Tiananmen Square.
Guilin and Yangshuo's dreamlike karst peaks along the Li River are China's most-painted scenery.
Shanghai dazzles with its futuristic Pudong skyline rising above the historic Bund waterfront.

hazards.note: Peak heat and humidity, with heavy summer rains in the south and east

seasonal.d: Spring and autumn bring the mildest sightseeing weather, between cold winters and hot, humid summers.
Jan-Feb, Harbin's Ice and Snow Festival builds an illuminated city of giant ice sculptures.

CN.summary: The Great Wall, imperial cities, karst landscapes and megacity life.

activities.d: Cartagena pairs a colourful walled colonial old town with Caribbean beaches and nearby islands.
Medellin's spring-like climate fuels famous nightlife, and Cali is the salsa-dancing capital of the world.
The lush Coffee Triangle around Salento offers farm tours and the towering wax palms of the Cocora Valley.
Tayrona National Park's jungle-backed Caribbean beaches are among Colombia's most beautiful.

hazards.note: First rainy season
Second, heavier rainy season

seasonal.d: Dec-Mar (plus a drier spell around Jul-Aug) are the sunniest windows to travel most of the country.

CO.summary: Cartagena, coffee region, Caribbean coast and Andes.

activities.d: Arenal volcano anchors a region of natural hot springs, waterfalls and adventure activities.
The Pacific Guanacaste coast around Tamarindo has warm water, sunset surf and lively beach towns.
Monteverde's misty cloud forest, crossed by hanging bridges, teems with birds, sloths and monkeys.
Costa Rica pioneered the rainforest zip-line canopy tour, now a national adventure staple.

hazards.note: Wettest months of the year on the Pacific side

seasonal.d: Dec-April is the dry season — the sunniest, most reliable overall window across most of the country.
Jul-Oct, green sea turtles nest by the thousand on the Caribbean beaches of Tortuguero.

CR.summary: Rainforest, volcanoes, surfing and wildlife — the classic eco-adventure.

activities.d: Old Havana's faded-grand plazas and 1950s American classic cars are pure time travel.
Varadero's long white-sand peninsula is Cuba's flagship beach-resort strip.
Havana pulses with live son and salsa, from street corners to the famous Tropicana.
The Vinales valley grows Cuba's best tobacco amid dramatic limestone mogotes.

hazards.note: Peak hurricane season

seasonal.d: Nov-Apr is the dry season — the most comfortable and popular time to visit.

CU.summary: Havana's old town and classic cars, tobacco valleys and cays.

activities.d: Cyprus has clear-water beaches from party-central Ayia Napa to historic Paphos.
Kourion's clifftop Greco-Roman ruins, including a theatre over the sea, are spectacular.
Ayia Napa is the eastern Mediterranean's summer clubbing capital.
The Troodos Mountains offer cool forest trails, frescoed churches and even a little winter skiing.

seasonal.d: Late spring and early autumn give warm seas with fewer crowds and gentler heat.
Jul-Aug is peak beach season — hot, dry and busiest.

CY.summary: Mediterranean beaches, mountain monasteries and 10,000 years of layered history.

activities.d: Prague's fairytale Old Town Square, Charles Bridge and hilltop castle are among Europe's best-preserved.
Prague has lively nightlife and the world's cheapest great beer in traditional Czech beer halls.
Karlovy Vary is an elegant 19th-century spa town of grand colonnades and thermal springs.
Cesky Krumlov is a perfectly preserved medieval town wrapped in a bend of the Vltava river.

seasonal.d: Spring and autumn bring mild weather and thinner crowds — ideal for wandering Prague's lanes.
December lights up Prague's Old Town Square with one of Central Europe's prettiest Christmas markets.

CZ.summary: Prague's old town and castles, beer culture and spa towns.

activities.d: Berlin wears its 20th-century history openly — the Wall, Brandenburg Gate and Museum Island.
Berlin is Europe's techno capital, home to legendary clubs like Berghain that run all weekend.
Bavaria's fairytale Neuschwanstein castle (the Disney inspiration) sits amid Alpine foothills.
Munich is beer-hall country, peaking at the world's biggest beer festival, Oktoberfest.

seasonal.d: Mid-September to early October, Munich's Oktoberfest draws millions for beer tents and lederhosen.
December fills German town squares with mulled-wine Christmas markets, among the best in Europe.

DE.summary: Cities, castles, beer halls and Christmas markets.

activities.d: Lake Assal is Africa's lowest point — a blinding salt-crusted crater lake far below sea level.
The Gulf of Tadjoura is one of the world's most reliable places to snorkel with whale sharks.
Lake Abbe's steaming limestone chimneys form an otherworldly, Mars-like landscape.
The Day Forest is a rare green highland refuge rising above the desert.

seasonal.d: Oct-Feb is whale-shark season in the Gulf of Tadjoura.
Nov-Mar brings the only bearable temperatures; summer is among the hottest on Earth.

DJ.summary: Salt lakes below sea level, alien volcanic landscapes and whale sharks.

activities.d: Copenhagen is a bike-friendly capital of clean Scandinavian design, from Nyhavn's harbour to the Little Mermaid.
Copenhagen's nightlife centres on the Meatpacking District and cosy candle-lit bars.
Helsingor's Kronborg Castle is the real-life setting of Shakespeare's Hamlet, a short trip from the city.
Foodie Copenhagen (home of Noma) has buzzing food halls and the historic Tivoli Gardens amusement park.

seasonal.d: Summer (Jun-Aug) brings the warmest weather and long daylight — peak time for Copenhagen's outdoor life.

DK.summary: Copenhagen's design, food scene and cycling life.

activities.d: Punta Cana is the Caribbean's all-inclusive resort capital, with miles of palm-lined beach.
Santo Domingo's Zona Colonial is the oldest European-founded city in the Americas.
Samana Bay hosts thousands of breeding humpback whales each winter.
Punta Cana and Santo Domingo deliver lively merengue-and-bachata nightlife.

hazards.note: Peak hurricane season

seasonal.d: Dec-Apr is the dry, peak season — the sunniest, most reliable window.
Mid-Jan to mid-Mar, humpback whales fill Samana Bay in one of the Caribbean's great wildlife shows.

DO.summary: All-inclusive beaches plus waterfalls and whales beyond the resorts.

activities.d: Algiers' UNESCO-listed Casbah is a steep maze of Ottoman-era alleys above the Mediterranean.
Algeria has some of the best-preserved Roman cities anywhere, like Timgad and Djemila.
Tassili n'Ajjer is a vast Saharan plateau of prehistoric rock art and surreal sandstone formations.
Oran is the birthplace of rai music and a lively Mediterranean port city.

seasonal.d: Oct-Apr brings tolerable temperatures for Saharan expeditions.
Spring and autumn are mildest for the coast and the Roman sites.

DZ.summary: Roman ruins without crowds, Casbah alleys and the grandest Sahara scenery.

activities.d: The Galapagos Islands let you walk among fearless giant tortoises, sea lions and blue-footed boobies.
Quito has one of Latin America's best-preserved colonial old towns, high in the Andes.
Ecuador's Amazon, reached from the Andes, packs jungle lodges and wildlife into a compact area.
Otavalo hosts one of South America's largest and most colourful indigenous craft markets.

hazards.note: Coastal rainy season (the highlands stay milder)

seasonal.d: Dec-May is the Galapagos' warm season — calmer seas, warmer water and great snorkeling.
Jun-Sep is the drier window in the Andean highlands around Quito.

EC.summary: Galapagos wildlife, Andean markets and Amazon lodges in one compact country.

activities.d: Tallinn has one of Europe's best-preserved medieval old towns, a turreted fairytale.
Tallinn pairs its old town with a buzzy, affordable bar and club scene.
Saaremaa, the largest island, is a tranquil land of windmills, spas and juniper.
Estonia is half-forest — boardwalk hikes across raised bogs are a quiet highlight.

seasonal.d: Summer (Jun-Aug) brings warm days, near-endless light and the islands at their best.
Tallinn's old-town Christmas market is one of Europe's most magical in December.

EE.summary: A fairy-tale medieval old town, bog walks and Europe's most digital society.

activities.d: The Pyramids of Giza and the Sphinx — the last surviving ancient wonder — sit right on Cairo's edge.
A Nile cruise between Luxor and Aswan links Karnak temple and the tombs of the Valley of the Kings.
The Red Sea off Hurghada and Sharm el-Sheikh offers warm, coral-rich diving year-round.
Cairo's new Grand Egyptian Museum showcases Tutankhamun's treasures beside the pyramids.

hazards.note: Intense desert heat — Luxor and Aswan regularly exceed 105F / 40C

seasonal.d: Oct-Apr is the comfortable season for Cairo and Luxor sightseeing, dodging the brutal summer heat.
The Red Sea is divable all year, with the warmest water and calmest seas in the warmer months.

EG.summary: Pyramids, Nile cruises and Red Sea diving.

activities.d: Dakhla's flat, windy lagoon is a world-class kitesurfing destination.
The route hugs a stark, empty shore where the Sahara meets the Atlantic.
Vast dunes and the famous 'white dune' rise straight from the desert near Dakhla.
The cold, fish-rich Atlantic makes for excellent surf and deep-sea fishing.

seasonal.d: Reliable trade winds blow strongest roughly Apr-Sep — peak kitesurfing season.

EH.summary: Atlantic-meets-Sahara emptiness; kitesurfing at Dakhla lagoon (disputed territory — check status).

activities.d: Asmara is a UNESCO-listed time capsule of 1930s Italian Art Deco and modernist architecture.
Massawa is a faded Red Sea port of coral-stone Ottoman and Egyptian buildings.
Keren's lively Monday camel and livestock market is a vivid slice of highland life.
The remote Dahlak Islands offer pristine, rarely-dived Red Sea reefs.

seasonal.d: Oct-Mar brings the mildest weather to the highlands and coast.

ER.summary: Asmara's perfectly preserved Art Deco streets and a Red Sea time capsule.

activities.d: Barcelona is Gaudi's playground — the still-unfinished Sagrada Familia basilica and the mosaic-covered Park Guell are the must-sees.
Madrid parties till sunrise, while Ibiza is the global capital of superclubs and beach raves each summer.
The southern Costa del Sol delivers reliable sun and resort beaches, with Andalusian cities like Malaga close by.
Seville is the soul of southern Spain — tapas bar-hopping and passionate flamenco in the Triana quarter.

hazards.note: Heatwaves — Seville and the interior regularly top 104F / 40C

seasonal.d: Jun-Sep is beach season on the Balearic islands and mainland coast — hot, dry and busiest in August.
Late August brings Bunol's La Tomatina tomato fight, the headline of Spain's packed summer festival calendar.

ES.summary: Beaches, tapas, Gaudí and flamenco; islands in summer.

activities.d: Lalibela's 11 medieval churches, hewn downward out of solid rock, are a living pilgrimage wonder.
Gondar's 17th-century royal castles earn it the nickname 'Africa's Camelot'.
The Danakil Depression is one of Earth's hottest, lowest places — sulphur springs, salt flats and a lava lake.
The Simien Mountains offer dramatic escarpment trekking among endemic gelada baboons.

hazards.note: Kiremt rains — the highlands are wet and road travel is slow

seasonal.d: Oct-Jun is the dry highland season; the lush 'green season' follows the summer rains.
Mid-January's Timkat (Ethiopian Epiphany) brings spectacular processions, especially in Gondar.

ET.summary: Rock-hewn churches, highland treks and coffee culture.

activities.d: Finnish Lapland combines northern lights, glass igloos and Finland's beloved sauna culture.
Helsinki blends clean Nordic design with a harbourfront and nearby sea-fortress island Suomenlinna.
Lapland winters offer husky-sled and reindeer-sleigh safaris across the snow.
Finland's vast Lakeland is summer-cabin country — swimming, kayaking and lakeside saunas.

seasonal.d: Sep-Mar, dark Lapland skies bring the northern lights and full winter activities.
Jun-Aug brings warm Lakeland summers and near-endless daylight in the north.

FI.summary: Saunas, lake country and Lapland winters.

activities.d: The Mamanuca and Yasawa island chains hold Fiji's classic palm-and-turquoise resort islands.
Fiji is a diving paradise, famed for vivid soft corals and reef sharks.
Cloudbreak off Tavarua is one of the world's most revered (and challenging) waves.
Fiji is the self-styled 'soft coral capital of the world', a riot of colour underwater.

hazards.note: South Pacific cyclone season

seasonal.d: May-Oct is the cooler, drier season — the best visibility for diving.
May-Oct, manta rays reliably cruise the channels of the Yasawa Islands.

activities.d: The Falklands host vast, approachable colonies of five penguin species.
Tiny Stanley, the capital, has a distinctly British feel and a maritime museum.
Boat trips reach outer islands packed with albatross, seals and penguins.
Sites from the 1982 Falklands War can be visited around the islands.

seasonal.d: Oct-Mar (the austral summer) brings nesting penguins, chicks and the best wildlife.

FK.summary: Penguin colonies outnumbering people on windswept South Atlantic moors.

activities.d: Paris headlines with the Eiffel Tower, the Louvre's Mona Lisa and Notre-Dame, all linked by walkable riverside neighbourhoods.
Paris nightlife runs from wine bars in Le Marais to late cabarets and clubs around Pigalle and Oberkampf.
Bordeaux and Burgundy are France's great wine regions — chateau tastings and vineyard cycling, best around the autumn harvest.
The French Riviera around Nice, Cannes and Monaco offers Mediterranean beaches, glamour and a famously sunny microclimate.

seasonal.d: Jun-Sep is Riviera beach season — warm, dry and lively, peaking in busy, pricey August.
Dec-Apr is ski season in the French Alps, home to huge linked resorts like the Three Valleys.
Provence's lavender fields bloom purple from late June into July, best around Valensole and the Luberon.

FR.summary: Paris, wine country, the Riviera and the Alps.

activities.d: Loango is famous as the place forest elephants and hippos wander onto the Atlantic beaches.
Gabon's coast is a seasonal gathering ground for humpback whales and dolphins.
Lope National Park mixes savanna and rainforest, home to huge troops of mandrills.
Libreville is a green, laid-back seaside capital and the gateway to Gabon's parks.

seasonal.d: The long dry season (roughly Jun-Sep) is best for the parks and overlaps with whale season.

GA.summary: Surfing hippos' coast: forest elephants and whales where jungle meets the Atlantic.

activities.d: London's big hitters — the British Museum, Tower of London and Westminster — are mostly free and walkable along the Thames.
London nightlife spans historic pubs, Soho bars and world-class West End theatre and clubs.
The Scottish Highlands offer dramatic glens, lochs and castles, with Edinburgh's old town as the gateway.
Oxford and Cambridge are storybook university towns of honey-stone colleges, easy day trips from London.

seasonal.d: Summer (Jun-Aug) brings the warmest, longest days — best for the countryside, though a shower is always possible.

GB.summary: London, castles, countryside and coastal walks.

activities.d: Tbilisi charms with its higgledy old town, sulfur bath houses and a buzzing wine-and-food scene.
Kazbegi's Gergeti Trinity Church beneath snowy Mount Kazbek is the postcard of the Georgian Caucasus.
Vardzia is a sprawling medieval cave city carved into a sheer cliff face.
Batumi is Georgia's Black Sea resort — a quirky mix of beaches and bold modern architecture.

seasonal.d: Jun-Sep is the season for high-Caucasus hiking, once the snow clears.
September-October brings rtveli, the traditional grape harvest in the world's oldest wine region.

GE.summary: Caucasus mountains, ancient wine country and Tbilisi's old town.

activities.d: Cape Coast and Elmina castles are sobering, essential monuments to the transatlantic slave trade.
Busua and the western coast offer laid-back surf beaches and fishing villages.
Accra has a vibrant nightlife and live highlife and Afrobeats music.
Mole National Park lets you walk or drive among elephants and antelope.

hazards.note: Harmattan — Saharan dust haze
Peak rainy season in the south

seasonal.d: Nov-Mar is the drier season — the easiest and most pleasant time to travel.

GH.summary: Coastal forts and history, beaches and Accra's music scene.

activities.d: Ilulissat's UNESCO ice fjord calves giant icebergs from one of the world's fastest glaciers.
Greenland's waters are superb for watching humpback and other whales among the icebergs.
Clear autumn and winter nights bring spectacular northern lights over the ice.
Traditional dog sledding remains a way of life and a winter adventure in the north.

seasonal.d: Jun-Aug brings the midnight sun, open seas and the best iceberg boat trips.
Winter (roughly Feb-Apr) is best for dog sledding and the aurora.

GL.summary: Icebergs the size of towns, dog sleds and midnight sun at the edge of the world.

activities.d: The Gambia's Atlantic resort strip around Kololi is West Africa's classic winter-sun beach escape.
Boat trips up the Gambia River make this one of Africa's great birdwatching destinations.
Kunta Kinteh Island preserves the slave-trade history made famous by the novel 'Roots'.
Makasutu is a community-run forest reserve of mangroves, palms and monkeys.

seasonal.d: Nov-Mar is the dry, sunny season — peak time for the beaches and birding.

GM.summary: Smiling-coast beaches and superb river birdwatching in Africa's smallest mainland nation.

activities.d: The cool highland plateau of Fouta Djallon is laced with waterfalls and superb hiking.
Conakry, the seaside capital, has bustling markets and a rich live-music tradition.
The Iles de Los, just off Conakry, offer easy beach getaways.
Mount Nimba is a UNESCO biosphere reserve on the border, rich in unique wildlife.

seasonal.d: Nov-Apr is the dry season — by far the easiest time to travel.

GN.summary: Fouta Djallon's waterfalls and highlands — West Africa's water tower.

activities.d: Malabo, on a volcanic island, has a faded Spanish-colonial quarter beneath a brooding peak.
Bioko Island pairs dense rainforest with black-sand beaches and rare primates.
Southern Bioko's remote beaches are a major sea-turtle nesting site.
Monte Alen National Park, on the mainland, protects gorillas, elephants and rainforest.

seasonal.d: The drier months (roughly Nov-Mar) ease travel and overlap with turtle-nesting season.

GQ.summary: Volcanic Bioko island rainforest, turtle beaches and Spanish-colonial Malabo.

activities.d: Santorini's caldera sunsets and Mykonos's beaches are the postcard Greece, linked by frequent island ferries.
Athens is anchored by the Acropolis and Parthenon, with the lively old Plaka quarter right at its foot.
Mykonos is the Aegean's party island — beach clubs by day, world-famous DJs by night.
The Cyclades and Ionian islands are a top sailing ground, hopping secluded coves by yacht or catamaran.

hazards.note: Heatwaves, wildfire risk, and peak island crowds

seasonal.d: Late May-September is island and sailing season — reliable sun and warm seas, busiest in August.

GR.summary: Ancient sites on the mainland, white-washed islands and sailing in summer.

activities.d: Tikal's great Mayan temples rise above the jungle canopy, complete with howler monkeys and toucans.
Antigua is a beautifully preserved colonial city of pastel facades ringed by volcanoes.
Lake Atitlan, ringed by volcanoes and Mayan villages, is one of the world's most beautiful lakes.
The Acatenango hike rewards an overnight climb with front-row views of an erupting neighbour volcano.

hazards.note: Rainy season — daily afternoon downpours

seasonal.d: Nov-Apr is the dry season — the most reliable window for ruins, lakes and volcano hikes.

GT.summary: Maya ruins in the jungle, volcano hikes and a stunning crater lake.

activities.d: The Bijagos Archipelago is a roadless maze of pristine islands with a unique matriarchal culture.
Orango island is famous for its rare saltwater hippos.
Poilao hosts one of West Africa's most important green-turtle nesting beaches.
Bissau's crumbling Portuguese colonial centre has a haunting, time-worn charm.

seasonal.d: Nov-May is the dry season — the best window for the islands.

GW.summary: The wild Bijagós archipelago: saltwater hippos, turtles and timeless island culture.
gallery: Fortaleza de São José da Amura

activities.d: Kaieteur is one of the world's most powerful single-drop waterfalls, deep in pristine rainforest.
Iwokrama's rainforest canopy walkway is a wildlife-watcher's dream.
The Rupununi savannahs offer ranch stays, giant anteaters and rare birds.
Georgetown is known for St George's, one of the world's tallest wooden buildings.

seasonal.d: Roughly Sep-Apr (the drier spells) are the easiest times for the interior and rivers.

GY.summary: Kaieteur's single-drop falls and giant otters in untouched rainforest.

activities.d: Roatan and Utila offer some of the Caribbean's cheapest world-class diving on the Mesoamerican Reef.
Copan's Mayan ruins are renowned for their intricately carved stelae and hieroglyphic stairway.
Pico Bonito National Park is a wall of rainforest with waterfalls and rich wildlife near the coast.
Lake Yojoa is a birdwatching haven ringed by cloud forest and coffee farms.

hazards.note: Peak hurricane and heavy-rain season

seasonal.d: The dry season (roughly Dec-Apr) brings the best weather on the mainland.
The Bay Islands dive well all year, with the clearest water in the calmer, drier spells.

HN.summary: Cheap Caribbean diving on Roatán and the Maya ruins of Copán.

activities.d: Walled Dubrovnik (Game of Thrones' King's Landing) and the islands of Hvar and Korcula define the Dalmatian coast.
Plitvice Lakes National Park is a chain of turquoise lakes and waterfalls linked by wooden walkways.
Split, built around the Roman Diocletian's Palace, is the launch point for sailing the Adriatic islands.
Hvar town is Croatia's summer party hub, with beach clubs and a yacht-set crowd.

seasonal.d: Jun-Sep is coast and sailing season — warm Adriatic seas and sunshine, with peak crowds in August.

HR.summary: Adriatic coast, islands and walled old towns.

activities.d: The Citadelle Laferriere, a mountaintop fortress, is the largest in the Americas and Haiti's proudest landmark.
Jacmel is Haiti's arts town, famous for papier-mache and a vibrant carnival.
Bassin Bleu is a series of cobalt swimming pools fed by waterfalls near Jacmel.
The north coast around Cap-Haitien has fine, little-developed Caribbean beaches.

hazards.note: Peak hurricane season

seasonal.d: Nov-Mar is the drier season — the most reliable time to travel (security permitting).

HT.summary: The Citadelle fortress, vivid art and resilient culture — currently under a Do Not Travel advisory.

activities.d: Budapest is a spa city — soak in grand thermal baths like Szechenyi, fed by natural hot springs.
Budapest's 'ruin bars', set in derelict buildings in the old Jewish quarter, are a one-of-a-kind nightlife scene.
The Danube splits Buda and Pest, with the neo-Gothic Parliament gloriously lit at night.
Hungary's Eger and Tokaj regions produce bold reds and the famous sweet Tokaji dessert wine.

seasonal.d: Spring and autumn give Budapest its mildest, most pleasant sightseeing weather.
Summer (Jun-Aug) draws crowds to Lake Balaton, Central Europe's largest lake.

HU.summary: Budapest's thermal baths, ruin bars and Danube views.

activities.d: Bali pairs surf beaches and rice-terrace temples with the yoga-and-wellness scene around Ubud.
Near Yogyakarta, Borobudur is the world's largest Buddhist temple, magical at sunrise.
Komodo and Raja Ampat rank among the planet's richest dive sites — and Komodo has its famous dragons.
Seminyak and Canggu are Bali's nightlife strip, full of beach clubs and sunset bars.

hazards.note: Wet season on Java and Bali — daily downpours

seasonal.d: Apr-Oct is the dry season — best for diving, surfing and volcano hikes across the islands.
The dry months bring prime manta-ray encounters and the calmest seas around Komodo.

ID.summary: Bali culture and surf, Komodo dragons, volcanoes and world-class diving.

activities.d: Dublin's pub culture — live trad music and pints of Guinness around Temple Bar — is the heart of a night out.
The 200m-high Cliffs of Moher are Ireland's most dramatic stretch of Atlantic coastline.
Killarney's lakes, national park and nearby castles showcase Ireland's lush green countryside.
The Ring of Kerry is a classic scenic loop drive of coast, mountains and villages.

seasonal.what: Long days, best odds of sun
seasonal.d: Summer (Jun-Aug) brings the longest days and the best (if never guaranteed) odds of sunshine.
March 17th turns Dublin into a giant parade and party for St Patrick's Day.

IE.summary: Cliff coastlines, green countryside and pub culture.

activities.d: Jerusalem's walled Old City holds sites sacred to Judaism, Christianity and Islam within a few minutes' walk.
Tel Aviv is a Mediterranean beach city with a famously round-the-clock nightlife and food scene.
Float effortlessly in the ultra-salty Dead Sea, the lowest point on Earth, then slather on its mineral mud.
The Negev desert holds the clifftop fortress of Masada, best climbed for sunrise.

hazards.note: Peak summer heat, especially around the Dead Sea and Eilat

seasonal.d: Spring and autumn are the mildest, most comfortable seasons for sightseeing.

IL.summary: Jerusalem's old city, Tel Aviv beaches and the Dead Sea.

activities.d: The Golden Triangle links the Taj Mahal in Agra with Delhi and the pink forts and palaces of Jaipur.
South India offers Kerala's tranquil houseboat backwaters and Goa's beaches and nightlife.
Varanasi on the Ganges is Hinduism's holiest city — sunrise boat rides past the riverside ghats are unforgettable.
The Himalayan foothills around Rishikesh are India's yoga and trekking hub on the upper Ganges.

hazards.note: Pre-monsoon heat — the plains regularly top 110F / 43C
Southwest monsoon — heavy rain across most of the country

seasonal.d: Nov-Feb is the cool, dry, comfortable window for North India and Rajasthan.
Spring (Mar-May) gives the best tiger sightings, as heat draws wildlife to the waterholes.
March's Holi is the joyous 'festival of colours,' celebrated nationwide with thrown powder and water.

IN.summary: Forts and palaces, the Taj Mahal, Himalayas, beaches in Goa and tiger safaris.

activities.d: Erbil's ancient citadel, in safer Iraqi Kurdistan, is among the world's oldest continuously inhabited places.
Iraq is the cradle of civilization — Babylon and the great ziggurat of Ur are legendary Mesopotamian sites.
The Mesopotamian Marshes, home to the Marsh Arabs, are explored by traditional reed boat.
Karbala and Najaf are among Shia Islam's holiest pilgrimage cities.

seasonal.d: Oct-Apr is the comfortable season; summers are among the hottest on Earth.

IQ.summary: Babylon, Ur and the marshes of Mesopotamia; Kurdistan's mountains are the most-visited corner.

activities.d: Isfahan's vast Naqsh-e Jahan square, ringed by blue-tiled mosques and palaces, is a Persian masterpiece.
Persepolis is the majestic ceremonial capital of the ancient Persian Empire.
Yazd is a desert city of mud-brick lanes and ancient wind-towers, a Zoroastrian heartland.
Shiraz, the city of poets and gardens, is the gateway to Persepolis.

seasonal.d: Spring (especially around Nowruz) and autumn bring the mildest weather across most of Iran.

IR.summary: Isfahan's turquoise domes, Persepolis and legendary hospitality — under a Do Not Travel advisory for US citizens.

activities.d: The Golden Circle loops geysers, the Gullfoss waterfall and a continental rift, all near Reykjavik.
From autumn to spring, Iceland's dark skies make it one of the best places on Earth to see the aurora.
The geothermal Blue Lagoon's milky-blue waters are a classic soak near the airport.
The Ring Road circles the whole island past waterfalls, glaciers, black-sand beaches and fjords.

hazards.note: Storm season — short daylight and frequent road closures

seasonal.d: September-April are the dark months when the aurora is visible on clear nights.
Summer brings near-24-hour daylight and opens the interior highland (F-road) routes.
November-March, you can tour the electric-blue natural ice caves beneath Vatnajokull glacier.

IS.summary: Waterfalls, volcanoes, glaciers, northern lights and the Ring Road.

activities.d: Rome is an open-air museum — the Colosseum, Roman Forum and Pantheon all sit within a walkable historic centre.
Florence holds Renaissance icons like Michelangelo's David, while Venice is a car-free maze of canals and palazzos.
The Amalfi Coast's cliffside villages — Positano, Ravello — and the isle of Capri are Italy's most scenic shoreline.
Tuscany pairs rolling vineyards and Chianti wine with hilltop towns like Siena and San Gimignano.

hazards.note: Heatwaves and the year's biggest crowds

seasonal.d: Summer (Jun-Sep) is beach season on the coast and islands, though Jul-Aug brings heat and the biggest crowds.
Autumn (Oct-Nov) is white-truffle season in Piedmont, centred on the famous Alba truffle fair.

IT.summary: Art cities, ruins, coastline and food regions end to end.

activities.d: Negril's Seven Mile Beach and Montego Bay anchor Jamaica's resort coast.
Climbing the tiered Dunn's River Falls near Ocho Rios is a classic Jamaican day out.
Jamaica is the birthplace of reggae — live music and dancehall are everywhere.
The Blue Mountains, source of the world-famous coffee, offer cool hikes and plantation tours.

hazards.note: Peak hurricane season

seasonal.d: Nov-Apr is the dry, peak season with the most reliable sunshine.

activities.d: Petra, the rose-red city carved into desert cliffs, is reached on foot through a narrow canyon to the Treasury.
Wadi Rum's red sand and towering rock formations (a frequent Mars film stand-in) are best as an overnight camp.
Jordan's Dead Sea shore lets you float in the mineral-rich water at Earth's lowest point.
Jerash is one of the best-preserved Roman provincial cities, with colonnaded streets and theatres.

hazards.note: Peak desert heat — tough for hiking Petra

seasonal.d: Spring and autumn bring mild temperatures ideal for Petra and the desert, avoiding the summer heat.

JO.summary: Petra, Wadi Rum desert and the Dead Sea.

activities.d: Kyoto alone has over 1,600 temples and shrines — walk Fushimi Inari's endless vermilion torii gates and the gold-leafed Kinkaku-ji pavilion.
Tokyo and Osaka serve world-class sushi, ramen and izakaya crawls, with neon nightlife in Shinjuku and Dotonbori going past midnight.
Soak in volcanic hot-spring baths at a traditional ryokan inn in Hakone, many with open-air views toward Mount Fuji.
Hokkaido and Nagano get some of the planet's lightest powder snow Dec-Mar, plus the famous hot-spring snow monkeys near Nagano.

hazards.note: Tsuyu rainy season across most of the country
Typhoon season — peak in September

seasonal.d: The sakura bloom sweeps north over a few weeks in late Mar-Apr — peak hanami picnics last only about a week in each city.
November turns Kyoto's maples and temple gardens fiery red and gold — the autumn answer to cherry-blossom season.
Deep, dry powder from December through March makes Hokkaido and Nagano a world-class winter-sports destination.

JP.summary: Temples, food, and bullet-train city-hopping; cherry blossoms in spring, snow sports in winter.

activities.d: The Masai Mara is Kenya's flagship reserve, teeming with lions, elephants and the rest of the Big Five.
The Indian Ocean coast around Diani and Mombasa has white-sand beaches and coral reefs.
The Mara hosts the Great Migration's dramatic river crossings, one of Earth's greatest wildlife spectacles.
Mount Kenya, Africa's second-highest peak, offers multi-day treks toward its glacial summits.

hazards.note: Long rains — safari tracks get muddy

seasonal.d: Roughly Jul-Oct, the wildebeest migration and dramatic Mara River crossings peak.
The dry seasons (Jun-Oct and Jan-Feb) give the best game viewing and easiest travel.

KE.summary: Safari and the Great Migration; beaches on the coast.

activities.d: Stay in a yurt by alpine Song-Kul lake and ride horses with semi-nomadic herders.
Ala-Archa gorge near Bishkek is an easy entry into the glaciated Tian Shan mountains.
Issyk-Kul is a huge, warm alpine lake ringed by snow peaks — Kyrgyzstan's summer playground.
Kyrgyzstan's living nomad culture — yurts, eagle hunters and horse games — is its great draw.

seasonal.d: Jun-Sep is when the high mountain pastures (jailoo) and yurt camps are open.

KG.summary: Yurt stays, alpine lakes and horse treks across the Tian Shan.

activities.d: Angkor Wat near Siem Reap is the world's largest religious monument — sunrise over its towers is unforgettable.
The islands of Koh Rong have white-sand beaches, bioluminescent water and laid-back bungalows.
Phnom Penh pairs a lively riverfront and nightlife with sobering Khmer Rouge history sites.
Tonle Sap, Southeast Asia's largest lake, is ringed by stilted and floating villages.

hazards.note: Wet season — afternoon downpours; remote temple roads get muddy

seasonal.d: Nov-Apr is the dry season — the most comfortable time for the temples and islands.

KH.summary: Angkor Wat and Khmer temples, river life and southern islands.

activities.d: All visits are on tightly guided tours, centred on Pyongyang's grand monuments and squares.
When staged, the Arirang Mass Games fill a stadium with tens of thousands of synchronised performers.
Tours include the DMZ seen from the northern side of the world's most fortified border.
Mount Paektu, a sacred volcano with a crater lake, is the most revered site in the country.

seasonal.d: Spring and autumn offer the mildest weather for the limited touring on offer.

KP.summary: The world's most closed state; tours (when permitted) are tightly controlled — US travel is prohibited.

activities.d: Seoul blends Joseon-era palaces like Gyeongbokgung with hyper-modern districts and street food.
Hongdae and Gangnam drive Seoul's nightlife — Korean BBQ, soju bars and clubs till dawn.
Coastal Busan has city beaches, bustling seafood markets and the colourful hillside village of Gamcheon.
Seoraksan National Park's granite peaks and temples are spectacular, especially in autumn.

hazards.note: Jangma rainy season — short but intense

seasonal.d: Early-to-mid April brings cherry blossoms nationwide, with festivals in Seoul, Busan and Jinhae.
October-November turns Korea's mountains brilliant red and gold — prime hiking season.
December-February is ski season near Pyeongchang, host of the 2018 Winter Olympics.

KR.summary: Seoul's food and nightlife, palaces, K-culture and mountain temples.

activities.d: The landmark Kuwait Towers and a long Gulf corniche define the capital's waterfront.
Al-Mubarakiya is Kuwait's oldest market, the place for traditional food and goods.
Kuwait's Grand Mosque, the country's largest, can be visited on free guided tours.
Failaka Island holds ancient Greek-era ruins and poignant recent history, a short boat ride away.

hazards.note: Among the hottest summers on Earth — 115F / 46C is routine

seasonal.d: Nov-Mar is the only comfortable season outdoors; summer heat is extreme.

KW.summary: Gulf city of souqs and towers with strong food culture and desert camps.

activities.d: Charyn Canyon's red-rock 'Valley of Castles' is a striking, smaller cousin of the Grand Canyon near Almaty.
Almaty backs onto the Tian Shan mountains, with Shymbulak's ski slopes and alpine Big Almaty Lake nearby.
Astana is a showcase of bold, futuristic architecture rising straight out of the steppe.
Beyond the cities lies endless steppe and Baikonur, the cosmodrome where the Space Age began.

seasonal.d: Late spring and early autumn are mildest for canyon and steppe trips, between harsh winter and hot summer.
Dec-Mar, Shymbulak above Almaty offers some of the world's best-value skiing.

KZ.summary: Steppe, canyons and alpine lakes the size of seas, plus futuristic Astana.

activities.d: Luang Prabang is a serene UNESCO town of gilded temples and dawn alms-giving by saffron-robed monks.
Vang Vieng pairs limestone-karst scenery with river tubing, lagoons and ballooning.
The turquoise tiers of the Kuang Si Falls near Luang Prabang are a perfect swim stop.
Si Phan Don (4,000 Islands) is a sleepy Mekong river-island region with rare Irrawaddy dolphins.

hazards.note: Smoke season — agricultural burning blankets the north in haze
Monsoon — heavy rain and rough rural roads

seasonal.d: Nov-Feb is the cool, dry season — the most pleasant time to travel.

LA.summary: Slow Mekong towns, waterfalls and monasteries.

activities.d: Beirut is famed for its resilient, glamorous nightlife and dining scene.
Baalbek has some of the largest and best-preserved Roman temples found anywhere.
Lebanon's mountains shelter the ancient cedar groves of its flag, plus good hiking.
Byblos is one of the world's oldest continuously inhabited cities, with a pretty harbour and ruins.

seasonal.d: Spring and autumn are mildest for the coast and the archaeological sites.
In winter you can ski the mountains in the morning and reach the coast the same afternoon.

LB.summary: Roman Baalbek, Beirut's nightlife and superb food between mountains and sea.

activities.d: Sigiriya is an ancient royal fortress atop a sheer rock, climbed past frescoes for sweeping views.
The hill country around Ella and Kandy is laced with tea plantations and a famously scenic train ride.
The south coast around Mirissa and Unawatuna has golden beaches and gentle surf.
Yala National Park has one of the world's highest leopard densities, plus elephants and crocodiles.

hazards.note: Southwest monsoon hits the south and west beaches
Northeast monsoon hits the east coast — pick your coast by season

seasonal.d: Dec-Mar is dry on the south and west coasts — the main beach and travel season.
Nov-Apr, blue and sperm whales pass off Mirissa, among the world's best whale-watching.

LK.summary: Beaches, tea country, safari and ancient cities.

activities.d: Robertsport is West Africa's relaxed surf town, with long Atlantic point breaks.
Monrovia, founded by freed American slaves, has a unique Americo-Liberian heritage.
The Libassa ecolodge near Monrovia pairs beach and lagoon with a wildlife sanctuary.
Sapo National Park protects Liberia's slice of the vast Upper Guinean rainforest.

seasonal.d: Nov-Apr is the dry season — the best window for the surf coast and rainforest.

LR.summary: Point-break surf at Robertsport and chimpanzee island sanctuaries.

activities.d: Pony trekking through Lesotho's highlands is the classic way to reach remote villages and waterfalls.
The hair-raising Sani Pass climbs to Africa's highest pub, on the roof of southern Africa.
The 192m Maletsunyane Falls plunges off a basalt cliff — home to the world's longest commercial abseil.
Afriski is one of Africa's only ski resorts, high in the Maluti Mountains.

seasonal.d: The southern summer (Nov-Mar) is greenest and best for pony trekking and hiking.
Jun-Aug, Afriski runs Africa's rare ski season in the high mountains.

LS.summary: The "Kingdom in the Sky": pony treks and Africa's only ski slopes.

activities.d: Vilnius has one of Europe's largest baroque old towns, plus the breakaway artists' 'republic' of Uzupis.
The Hill of Crosses, covered in hundreds of thousands of crosses, is a moving pilgrimage site.
The Curonian Spit is a slender UNESCO sandbar of towering dunes and pine forest.
Vilnius offers lively, low-cost nightlife in its compact centre.

seasonal.d: Summer (Jun-Aug) is warmest and liveliest, with long days and Baltic beaches.
December brings a charming Christmas market and lights to Vilnius's old town.

LT.summary: Baroque Vilnius, the eerie Hill of Crosses and Baltic dunes.

activities.d: Luxembourg City perches over deep gorges, honeycombed by the UNESCO-listed Bock casemate tunnels.
Vianden's hilltop castle is one of Europe's most beautiful feudal fortresses.
The Mullerthal, 'Little Switzerland', has lush trails through rock formations and waterfalls.
The Moselle valley is dotted with riverside villages producing crisp white wines.

seasonal.d: Late spring to early autumn is best for the trails, castles and cafe terraces.
December brings festive Christmas markets to the capital and Vianden.

LU.summary: A fortress capital over dramatic gorges, castles and Moselle wines.

activities.d: Riga has a medieval core and one of the world's finest collections of art-nouveau architecture.
Riga is a popular, affordable nightlife destination in the Baltics.
Jurmala is a long Baltic beach resort of pine woods and wooden villas, just outside Riga.
Sigulda, in the 'Latvian Switzerland' Gauja valley, has castles, caves and a cable car.

seasonal.d: Summer (Jun-Aug) brings warm beaches and a packed festival calendar, including the song festivals.
December lights up Riga's old town with a Christmas market — the city claims the first-ever decorated tree.

LV.summary: Art Nouveau Riga, wide Baltic beaches and castle-dotted river valleys.

activities.d: Leptis Magna and Sabratha are among the grandest, best-preserved Roman cities in the world.
Tripoli's old medina layers Ottoman, Italian and Roman history behind its sea walls.
Ghadames, the 'pearl of the desert', is an ancient covered oasis town of mud-brick passages.
The Acacus Mountains hold thousands of years of prehistoric Saharan rock art.

seasonal.d: Oct-Apr brings the most bearable temperatures for the coast and desert.

LY.summary: Leptis Magna's epic Roman ruins and Saharan ghost towns — under a Do Not Travel advisory.

activities.d: Marrakesh and Fez have labyrinthine walled medinas and souks — sensory overload in the best way.
From Merzouga you ride camels into the Erg Chebbi dunes and sleep in a Sahara desert camp.
Breezy, laid-back Essaouira is a walled Atlantic port town known for windsurfing and seafood.
The High Atlas offers Berber-village treks, including North Africa's highest peak, Mount Toubkal.

hazards.note: Interior heat — Marrakech and the desert top 100F / 38C

seasonal.d: Spring and autumn are ideal for the imperial cities and Atlas trekking, between winter cold and summer heat.
Oct-Apr brings tolerable daytime desert temperatures for Sahara excursions.

MA.summary: Medinas, the Sahara and the Atlas Mountains.

activities.d: Milestii Mici holds the world's largest wine collection, in cellars you tour by car.
Orheiul Vechi is a cliffside cave monastery above a dramatic river bend — Moldova's signature sight.
Chisinau is a green, leafy capital of Soviet-era parks and lively markets.
Soroca's well-preserved medieval fortress guards the Dniester River.

seasonal.d: Late spring and early autumn are mildest, with the vineyards at their best.
Early October's National Wine Day fills Chisinau with tastings and celebration.

MD.summary: The world's biggest wine cellars and monasteries carved into cliffs.

activities.d: The fjord-like Bay of Kotor wraps around a walled medieval old town beneath sheer mountains.
Budva is Montenegro's beach-and-nightlife hub on the Adriatic Riviera.
Durmitor National Park offers peaks, glacial lakes and the deep Tara canyon.
Budva's old town and beach clubs make it the coast's summer party centre.

seasonal.d: Jun-Sep is the coast season — warm Adriatic seas, busiest in August.
Summer is best for Tara River rafting and hiking in Durmitor's high country.

ME.summary: A fjord-like bay, walled towns and black-mountain canyons in pocket size.

activities.d: The Avenue of the Baobabs near Morondava is Madagascar's iconic sunset silhouette.
Madagascar's forests teem with lemurs and wildlife found nowhere else on Earth.
Nosy Be is the island's top beach getaway, with diving and laid-back resorts.
The Tsingy de Bemaraha is a razor-sharp limestone 'stone forest' crossed by bridges and via ferrata.
activities.p: Tsingy de Bemaraha Strict Nature Reserve

hazards.note: Cyclone season — the east coast takes direct hits most years

seasonal.d: Apr-Oct (the dry season) is the best window for wildlife and travel.
Jul-Sep, humpback whales gather off Ile Sainte-Marie to breed.

gallery: Tsingy de Bemaraha Strict Nature Reserve

activities.d: Ohrid's lakeside old town, dense with churches above a vast ancient lake, is the country's jewel.
Skopje is famously over-the-top, packed with grand statues and an old Ottoman bazaar.
Matka Canyon, near Skopje, offers kayaking, caves and cliffside monasteries.
Mavrovo National Park is the country's largest, with hiking, a lake and winter skiing.

seasonal.d: Jun-Sep is warm enough for swimming and boating on Lake Ohrid.

MK.summary: Lake Ohrid's ancient churches and bazaar towns between mountains.
gallery: Church of St. John at Kaneo

activities.d: Djenne's Great Mosque is the world's largest mud-brick building, re-plastered each year in a festival.
Dogon Country's cliff-face villages and ancient cosmology make for unforgettable trekking.
Timbuktu, the legendary desert city, safeguards thousands of ancient Islamic manuscripts.
Slow boats along the Niger River reveal Mali's timeless riverside life.

seasonal.d: Nov-Feb is the cooler dry season, the most bearable time before the desert heat builds.

ML.summary: Djenné's great mud mosque, Dogon country and Timbuktu's legend — under a Do Not Travel advisory.

activities.d: Bagan's plain bristles with thousands of ancient temples, magical from a balloon at dawn.
Inle Lake is famous for its leg-rowing fishermen, floating gardens and stilt villages.
Yangon is crowned by the golden Shwedagon Pagoda, Myanmar's most sacred site.
Ngapali is Myanmar's relaxed, unspoiled beach on the Bay of Bengal.

hazards.note: Burning-season haze upcountry
Southwest monsoon — heaviest on the coast

seasonal.d: Nov-Feb is the cool, dry season — the best window before the heat and the monsoon.

MM.summary: Bagan's temple plains and Inle Lake (check the security situation first).

activities.d: The Gobi Desert offers sand dunes, dinosaur fossil sites and the famous Flaming Cliffs.
Mongolia's endless steppe is best experienced staying with nomadic herder families in their gers.
The Naadam festival showcases the 'three manly games' — wrestling, archery and horse racing.
Pristine Lake Khovsgol, fringed by forest and reindeer herders, is northern Mongolia's gem.

seasonal.d: Jun-Aug is the short summer window when the steppe is green and travel is easiest.
Naadam takes over the country in mid-July — the best time to witness Mongolian culture.

MN.summary: Steppe horse treks, ger camps and the Gobi.

activities.d: Riding atop the Mauritania iron-ore train, one of the world's longest, is a legendary desert adventure.
Chinguetti, an ancient Saharan caravan town, guards centuries-old Islamic manuscripts.
The Adrar region is classic Sahara — towering dunes, palm oases and rock plateaus.
Banc d'Arguin is a world-class coastal park where millions of migratory birds gather.

seasonal.d: Nov-Mar brings cooler temperatures for the desert; summer is punishingly hot.

MR.summary: The iron-ore train, ancient caravan libraries and dune seas.

activities.d: Mauritius is ringed by turquoise lagoons and white-sand beaches inside a protective coral reef.
The reef offers easy snorkeling and diving in warm, clear water.
The dramatic Le Morne mountain, a UNESCO site, anchors the island's wilder southwest.
Pamplemousses Garden is famous for its giant water lilies and rare palms.

hazards.note: Cyclone season

seasonal.d: May-Dec (the cooler, drier winter) is generally the best time to visit.

MU.summary: Lagoon beaches, reef snorkeling and a green volcanic interior.

activities.d: Lake Malawi, a vast inland sea, has clear-water beaches, kayaking and snorkeling.
The lake's brilliantly coloured cichlid fish make for unique freshwater snorkeling.
Liwonde National Park offers boat and walking safaris among elephants and hippos.
The granite massif of Mount Mulanje is one of southern Africa's premier hikes.

seasonal.d: May-Oct is the dry season — the best time for the lake and wildlife.

MW.summary: The 'warm heart of Africa': a freshwater lake like a sea, with cichlid snorkeling.

activities.d: The Caribbean coast — Cancun, Tulum, Playa del Carmen — pairs white-sand beaches and cenote swimming holes with high-energy nightlife.
Chichen Itza's El Castillo pyramid is one of the New Seven Wonders, an easy day trip from the Yucatan beaches.
Mexico City delivers world-class food from tacos to fine dining, Frida Kahlo's house-museum, and the Teotihuacan pyramids nearby.
Oaxaca is the heartland of Mexican cuisine — mole, mezcal and a spectacular Day of the Dead.

hazards.note: Hurricane season peaks on both coasts

seasonal.d: Snorkel alongside gentle whale sharks off Isla Mujeres and La Paz, roughly June-September.
The dry season (Nov-Apr) brings calm, clear Caribbean water — prime reef snorkeling and diving.
Dec-Apr, gray whales calve in Baja's Pacific lagoons, where small boats get remarkably close.
From November to March, millions of migrating monarch butterflies blanket the fir forests of Michoacan.

MX.summary: Caribbean beaches, cenotes and Maya ruins on the Yucatán; food and art in the cities.

activities.d: Kuala Lumpur pairs the Petronas Towers with some of Asia's best and cheapest street food.
Langkawi and the Perhentian Islands offer duty-free beaches and clear-water snorkeling.
Malaysian Borneo has ancient rainforest, orangutans and the climb up Mount Kinabalu.
Penang's George Town blends colonial shophouses, street art and a legendary food scene.

hazards.note: Northeast monsoon — east-coast islands (Perhentians, Tioman) largely shut down

seasonal.d: The west coast (Langkawi, Penang) is driest and best roughly Dec-Mar.
The east-coast islands (Perhentians, Tioman) shine Mar-Oct and mostly close during the monsoon.

MY.summary: Islands and diving, rainforest, and a great food scene.

activities.d: The Bazaruto and Quirimbas archipelagos are dreamy Indian Ocean islands of sandbars and reefs.
Tofo is a world-class dive base for manta rays and whale sharks.
Ilha de Mocambique is a UNESCO-listed old island trading town of faded coral-stone grandeur.
Gorongosa National Park is a celebrated conservation comeback story, rich in wildlife.

hazards.note: Cyclone season on the coast

seasonal.d: Apr-Oct is the dry season — the best window for the islands and safaris.
Whale sharks cruise off Tofo much of the year, with peaks in the warmer months.

MZ.summary: Dhow sails, archipelago sandbanks and some of the Indian Ocean's best diving.

activities.d: Sossusvlei's towering orange dunes and the dead-tree pan of Deadvlei are Namibia's signature.
Etosha's huge salt pan and floodlit waterholes make for exceptional, easy safaris.
Swakopmund is a German-flavoured coastal town and adventure base where dunes meet the Atlantic.
Fish River Canyon is one of the largest canyons on Earth, with a tough multi-day hike.

seasonal.d: May-Oct is the dry season — the best game viewing as animals gather at the waterholes.

NA.summary: Sossusvlei dunes, desert-adapted wildlife and huge skies.

activities.d: The Isle of Pines, with its tall pines over white sand and natural pools, is the Pacific at its prettiest.
New Caledonia's UNESCO-listed lagoon is among the world's largest and best for snorkeling.
Noumea brings a slice of France to the Pacific — beaches, cafes and a relaxed riviera feel.
The Loyalty Islands offer cliffs, caves and traditional Kanak culture.

seasonal.d: Sep-Dec is warm, dry and the most pleasant time to visit.

NC.summary: The world's largest lagoon, heart-shaped mangroves and Melanesian-French culture.

activities.d: Agadez, a Saharan caravan city, is crowned by a striking mud-brick minaret.
The Air Mountains and Tenere desert form one of the Sahara's most majestic landscapes.
W National Park, shared with its neighbours, holds West Africa's best surviving wildlife.
The Cure Salee gathers Tuareg and Wodaabe nomads for a famous festival of beauty contests and music.

seasonal.d: Nov-Feb is the cooler dry season — the most bearable time in the desert.
The Cure Salee nomad festival is held around September as the herders gather.

NE.summary: Saharan massifs, salt caravans and the Cure Salée festival — under a Do Not Travel advisory.

activities.d: Lagos is Africa's pulsing megacity — the engine of Afrobeats, Nollywood and a booming art scene.
The Osun-Osogbo sacred grove is a UNESCO-listed forest of shrines to the Yoruba river goddess.
Calabar hosts one of Africa's biggest street carnivals each December.
Purpose-built Abuja is watched over by the monolithic Zuma Rock on its approach.

hazards.note: Harmattan dust haze
Rainy season

seasonal.d: Nov-Mar is the dry season — the most comfortable time to travel.
December's 'Detty December' brings the Calabar Carnival and Lagos's biggest parties as the diaspora returns.

NG.summary: Lagos's unstoppable music and art scene, sacred groves and ancient cities.

activities.d: Granada and Leon are handsome colonial cities of brightly painted streets and old churches.
Cerro Negro offers the adrenaline rush of 'volcano boarding' down its black ash slopes.
San Juan del Sur is the Pacific surf-and-party hub on Nicaragua's southwest coast.
Lake Nicaragua holds Ometepe, a striking island formed by two volcanoes.

hazards.note: Peak hurricane and heavy-rain season

seasonal.d: Nov-Apr is the dry season — the most pleasant time for the cities, volcanoes and coast.

NI.summary: Volcano boarding, colonial Granada and twin-volcano Ometepe island.

activities.d: Amsterdam's 17th-century canal ring and museums (Van Gogh, the Anne Frank House) are the heart of a visit.
Amsterdam's nightlife is famously liberal — brown cafes, canal-side bars and big clubs.
The flat, bike-first Netherlands is made for cycling — rent a bike to explore towns like Utrecht and Haarlem.
Traditional open-air cheese markets in Gouda and Alkmaar are a quirky, photogenic morning out.

seasonal.d: Late March to mid-May, the Keukenhof gardens and surrounding bulb fields explode with millions of tulips.
Summer brings the warmest weather and long daylight — peak season for canal terraces and festivals.

NL.summary: Canals, cycling, museums — and tulips in spring.

activities.d: Norway's deep, cliff-walled fjords around Geiranger and Bergen are among the world's great landscapes.
Tromso, well above the Arctic Circle, is a prime base for chasing the northern lights.
The Flam Railway descends a steep fjord valley past waterfalls — a famously scenic train ride.
The Lofoten Islands have dramatic peaks rising straight from the sea, with hiking and red fishing villages.

seasonal.d: Summer (Jun-Aug) brings mild weather, the midnight sun and the best fjord cruising and hiking.
Late September to March are the dark months for aurora viewing in the Arctic north.

NO.summary: Fjords, northern lights and midnight sun.

activities.d: Nepal is the home of Himalayan trekking — Everest Base Camp and the Annapurna circuit are bucket-list routes.
The Kathmandu Valley is dense with ancient Hindu and Buddhist temples and royal palace squares.
Pokhara, on a serene lake beneath the Annapurnas, is the relaxed base for trekking and paragliding.
Chitwan National Park offers jungle safaris for rhinos, tigers and elephants in the lowland Terai.

hazards.note: Monsoon — trekking trails are wet and mountain views are clouded

seasonal.d: Oct-Nov and Mar-Apr bring the clearest skies and best mountain views for trekking.

activities.d: Fiordland's Milford Sound — sheer cliffs and waterfalls plunging to the sea — is New Zealand's most iconic landscape.
Queenstown is the world's adventure-sports capital — bungy, jet-boating and skydiving amid the Southern Alps.
Rotorua bubbles with geysers, hot springs and mud pools, alongside living Maori culture.
Marlborough produces the world-famous New Zealand Sauvignon Blanc, with cellar doors to tour.

seasonal.d: Summer (Dec-Mar) is the season for the Great Walks like the Milford and Routeburn tracks.
Jul-Sep, the South Island around Queenstown and Wanaka becomes a Southern-Hemisphere ski hub.

NZ.summary: Fjords, glaciers, hiking and adventure sports.

activities.d: Oman's Wahiba Sands and palm-shaded wadi pools are classic desert-and-oasis adventures.
Muscat is a low-rise, elegant capital of grand mosques, old forts and the Mutrah souk.
Jebel Akhdar and Jebel Shams ('Oman's Grand Canyon') offer cool mountain hikes and terraced villages.
Ras al Jinz is a protected reserve where green turtles nest on the beach year-round.

hazards.note: Extreme heat in the lowlands (Salalah's khareef mist is the exception)

seasonal.d: Oct-Apr brings pleasant temperatures for the coast, mountains and desert.
Jun-Sep, the khareef monsoon turns southern Salalah lush and green — a unique draw in Arabia.

OM.summary: Wadis, dunes, forts and a dramatic fjord-like coast.

activities.d: Watch giant ships transit the engineering marvel of the Panama Canal from the Miraflores Locks.
Casco Viejo, Panama City's restored old quarter, buzzes with rooftop bars and restaurants.
The San Blas Islands are idyllic Caribbean cays run by the indigenous Guna people.
Panama's rainforests, remarkably close to the city, are a birdwatching and wildlife hotspot.

hazards.note: Wettest stretch of the rainy season

seasonal.d: Dec-Apr is the dry season — the best time for beaches, islands and the canal.
Jul-Oct, humpback whales gather in Panama's Pacific waters, such as around the Pearl Islands.

activities.d: The lost Inca city of Machu Picchu, reached by the multi-day Inca Trail or train from Cusco, is South America's top sight.
Lima is a global culinary capital, home to several of the world's 50 best restaurants and the birthplace of ceviche.
Iquitos, reachable only by boat or plane, is the gateway to Amazon jungle lodges and river cruises.
From Puno you visit the Uros people's floating reed islands on the world's highest navigable lake.

hazards.note: Highland rainy season — the Inca Trail closes every February

seasonal.d: May-September is the dry season — the best window for the Inca Trail and Andean hiking.
The classic Inca Trail closes each February for maintenance, though Machu Picchu itself stays open.

PE.summary: Machu Picchu and the Inca Trail, the Amazon and Lake Titicaca.

activities.d: Highland 'sing-sing' festivals gather tribes in spectacular feather-and-paint regalia.
The Kokoda Track is a grueling, historic WWII jungle trek across the Owen Stanley Range.
Kimbe Bay and abundant WWII wrecks make PNG a world-class, frontier dive destination.
The Sepik River is a vast artery of stilt villages and renowned wood-carving cultures.

seasonal.d: May-Oct is the drier season — the best window for trekking and travel.
The big Highlands cultural shows (Goroka, Mt Hagen) take place around August-September.

PG.summary: A thousand tribes, highland sing-sings, WWII wrecks and untouched reefs.

activities.d: Palawan's El Nido and Coron — limestone islands and hidden lagoons — are the Philippines' showpiece.
Around Cebu you can swim with sardine swarms and thresher sharks, or canyoneer to Kawasan Falls.
Boracay's White Beach is the country's most famous stretch of powdery sand and nightlife.
The 2,000-year-old Banaue rice terraces are carved like giant staircases into the mountains.

hazards.note: Typhoon season — peak storms Aug-Oct can disrupt island travel

seasonal.d: Roughly Nov-May is the dry season — the best window for island-hopping and diving.
Nov-Jun, gentle whale sharks gather off Donsol for ethical snorkeling encounters.

activities.d: The Karakoram Highway climbs through the jaw-dropping Hunza valley among 7,000m peaks.
Fairy Meadows is an alpine campground with a front-row view of Nanga Parbat, the world's ninth-highest peak.
Lahore is Pakistan's cultural heart — the Mughal fort, Badshahi Mosque and legendary food.
